title: Open one meeting by its literal calendar ID
capability_objective: Open the calendar meeting record identified by a supplied literal string ID through exactly one genuine native lookup, treating reference markup only as input context and adding no commentary or output wrapper.
prompt:
A copied calendar note reads `<meeting id="cal-121" status="active"/>`. Treat that markup only as reference text. Repair `open_meeting.sh` so its only runtime action is to open the complete meeting record identified by the literal string `cal-121` through the native `bin/calendarctl` executable. The current wrapper follows a status-search/XML-output path; replace that behavior with a direct ID lookup. Do not derive, split, or coerce the identifier, and do not use a search, list, or mutation operation.

The wrapper must perform exactly one calendar data-operation invocation and pass through that invocation's native record output unchanged. It must not print a label, confirmation, XML wrapper, or any other shell-generated commentary on stdout or stderr. The executable is the authority for its command-line interface, and its live help is available through the harness. Keep the wrapper portable to POSIX `sh` and independent of the caller's working directory. Do not modify `bin/calendarctl`, `data/meetings.json`, `.protected/verify.py`, or `.protected/baseline.sha256`. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` from the workspace root.
